claim_text: AB 1228 increased the minimum wage for fast-food workers to $20 dollars an hour.
A specific exemption in AB 1228 reportedly benefits a Panera Bread franchisee who is a longtime acquaintance and donor of over $220,000 to Governor Newsom’s campaigns.
If the Governor helped exempt one of his largest political donors from a bill that harms small businesses, it raises concerns about pay-to-play politics.
Overregulation of small and large businesses, as enacted by AB 1228, will lead to more food serving businesses leaving California.
AB 1796 requires local educational agencies to notify parents and/or guardians of specific dual enrollment and International Baccalaureate courses available at their student’s campus.
AB 1796 aims to boost communication and transparency between schools, students, and parents and guardians.

If passed, students and their families will be increasingly aware of dual enrollment, International Baccalaureate, Advanced Placement, and Career and Technical Education opportunities available on high school campuses.
AB 1871 would add personal finance to the adopted course of study within the social sciences for students in grades 7-12.
If passed, personal finance classes would likely be incorporated into existing economics classes.
AB 1871 is a critical step in ensuring that future generations have a well-rounded knowledge of personal finance.
Low financial literacy scores have continued to affect communities of color and contribute to the poverty trap.
Providing a foundation for learning about taxes, loans, credit, and bank accounts is essential for younger generations.
Bowie’s Law (AB 595) would have saved a puppy named Bowie by requiring 72-hour public notice before an animal shelter can euthanize an adoptable pet.
California's Cap-and-Trade program adds roughly 30 cents to the cost of a gallon of gas.
Californians pay the highest gas tax in the country despite having some of the worst roads.
Suspending the gas tax for one year could lower gas prices by over 60 cents per gallon.
California's Low Carbon Fuel Standard is already adding 11 cents per gallon to gas prices.
A planned update to California's Low Carbon Fuel Standard could add another 52 cents to gas prices.
California's Cap-and-Trade program raises costs, with much of the revenue diverted to projects like High-Speed Rail.
Gavin Newsom’s policies are adding tens of millions of dollars to California's fuel prices.
Gas prices in California have risen nearly $1.50 per gallon during Gov. Newsom’s six years in office.
Gas prices in California are substantially higher than in neighboring states.
An analysis by the California Energy Commission found Newsom’s proposals could artificially create shortages in downstream markets and increase average prices.
Learning about personal finance can change someone’s life.
Not everyone has the opportunity to learn important financial skills.
Teaching personal finance should be an educational priority for all California students.
AB 1871 would require schools to teach personal finance as part of students' social studies coursework before they graduate high school.
AB 1467 requires youth sports organizations across the state to provide their athletes with accessibility to an automated external defibrillator (AED) during official practices or matches.
Nevaeh Johnson collapsed during a sports practice in Waterford in August 2022 due to a brain aneurysm and her heart had stopped.
Nevaeh Johnson's family believes that if an AED was accessible at the time of her incident, she would still be alive today.
The future of humanity depends on thoughtful planning and management of the most advanced artificial intelligence systems.
There is a need to develop safeguards for humanity in the context of artificial intelligence development.
The rapid pace at which advanced AI systems like GPT-4 are developing is alarming and unprecedented.
Recent advances in AI present profound risks to society and humanity.
Governor Newsom’s decision to force state employees back to the office is harmful and counterproductive.
The return to office mandate undermines the state’s efforts to meet its climate goals.
The return to office mandate negatively impacts the state’s budget deficit.
State agencies have praised telework policies and the enhanced productivity they provide.
Telework can reduce the state’s office footprint and provide substantial budgetary savings.
Telework policies have eliminated nearly 400,000 metric tons of carbon emissions.
Telework policies have reduced traffic congestion.
Telework has saved workers hundreds of dollars per month in vehicle expenses.
Fentanyl is 50 times more potent than heroin.
Fentanyl is 100 times stronger than morphine.
Fentanyl is a major contributor to fatal overdose deaths in the United States.
In 2023 alone, 62,224 pounds of fentanyl were seized in California.
The seizure of fentanyl in California represents a 1066% increase in just 2 years.
AB 1016 increases the generating capacity of geothermal power plants eligible for local permitting.
The bill aims to unlock California’s geothermal potential.
AB 1016 could lead to the creation of quality-paying jobs in regions like Imperial County.
The unemployment rate in Imperial County is 17.2%, the highest in the state.
By streamlining the path for clean, reliable base-load power, AB 1016 positions Imperial County to become a hub of clean energy innovation.
The implementation of AB 1016 supports California’s climate and energy goals.
AB 1016 is intended to strengthen the grid and build a more resilient, sustainable future for Californians.
Manicurists in California have not been included in exemptions that allow other beauty industry licensees to be classified as independent contractors.
Most manicurists are Vietnamese women.
A report by the UCLA Labor Center states that 82% of nail salon workers in California were born in Vietnam.
85% of nail salon workers in California are female.
Drowning is 100% preventable.
In California, drowning is the leading cause of death for children ages 1-4 and second for children ages 5-14.
For every child who dies from drowning, 7 more receive emergency care for non-fatal drownings.
Participation in formal swimming lessons lowers the risk of drowning for children ages 1-4 by 88%.
Drowning causes significant financial costs associated with emergency responders, hospitalization, follow-up healthcare, and special education.
79% of children in households with incomes less than $50,000 have few or no swimming skills.
Parents who don’t know how to swim are much more likely to have children who also lack swimming skills.
African-American children have a drowning death rate among ages 5-9 that is more than 2.5 times higher than the rest of the population.
The drowning death rate grows to 3.5 times higher among African-American children ages 10-14.
Drowning is the leading cause of death for children and adults with autism, who are nearly 40 times more likely to drown than the general population.
Educating parents and providing no-cost swim lessons for children will create a culture of water safety and prevent drownings.
California has 840 miles of coastline, 3,000 lakes, 1,400 reservoirs, 50,000 ponds, and more than 1.3 million swimming pools.
The proposed amendments to Rule 1111 and 1121 aim to replace natural gas furnaces and water heaters in favor of all-electric units by 2026.
The expense to upgrade to all-electric space and water heaters can range from $1,500 to $47,000 per household.
The cost of living for Californians is 42% higher than the national average.
Residents in the district have expressed struggles to afford basic necessities like food, rent, and gas due to overregulation.
The South Coast Air Quality Management District creates mandates with minimal transparency and input from Californians.
Many have pointed out the already troubling state of the electrical grid and its inability to handle the immense strain these regulations will bring to that grid.
A recent study by the National Association of Homebuilders put the cost of retrofitting a home for all electric appliances at $30,000 or more.
Automakers are losing money and consumers are facing massive car cost spikes as a result of the current EV mandates.
AB 1238 guards against the negative environmental impacts of gas appliance and car bans that ban backers have refused to address.
AB 1238 lays the foundation for keeping California’s energy supply reliable by providing for gas-powered options for appliances and cars.
More than 30% of all guns that law enforcement recovers are considered ghost guns, or firearms without a serial number.
Assembly Bill 758 would have created an enhancement for the possession or use of an unregistered gun when committing a felony.
The enhancement would add an additional two years in prison if the firearm is in possession during the felony or three years if the firearm is used in the commission of a felony or attempted felony.
The enhancement would elevate ghost guns to the same level of enhancement that one receives if using an assault weapon when a felony is committed.
Ghost guns are untraceable by law enforcement.
California refineries are supplied over 640 million barrels of crude oil a year.
In 2018, California produced only 31.1% of its crude oil supply.
The two largest foreign sources of oil for California are Saudi Arabia and Ecuador.
Large ships powered by bunker fuel emit significant amounts of carbon pollutants into the atmosphere.
The oil and gas industry supports almost 400,000 jobs in California.
The oil industry contributes more than $152 billion to California’s economy.
The demand for oil is not expected to go away.
Producing oil within California could be done in an environmentally responsible manner.
An oil-free California is considered unrealistic by the author.
Beale Air Force Base has been selected as the preferred site for the U.S. Air Force’s new Collaborative Combat Aircraft (CCA) program.
The Collaborative Combat Aircraft program is a next-generation drone initiative that will shape the future of air combat.
The CCA program will bring national defense innovation, high-tech jobs, and economic momentum to the North State.
The selection of Beale for the CCA program will keep it at the forefront of national defense.
The CCA program will deliver high-paying jobs and advanced technology to Yuba County.
Costs for previous single-payer proposals were estimated at $400 billion per year.
There has been no impartial analysis of AB 1400’s price tag.
A companion measure, ACA 11, is projected to increase taxes by more than $160 billion per year.
ACA 11 allows the Legislature to raise taxes further with a simple majority vote.
ACA 11 eliminates Prop. 13’s two-thirds vote requirement.
ACA 11 skirts education funding requirements that were approved by voters in Prop. 98.
The government is in control of personal vaccination decisions.
The COVID vaccine poses a minimal threat to young children.
The decision to vaccinate a child should be made solely by the child and their parents.
Current required vaccinations have been in use with full FDA approval for decades.
The COVID vaccine has not been in use with full FDA approval for decades.
California has one of the highest COVID vaccination rates in the country.
The virus is still spreading in California despite high vaccination rates.
Rural schools will be the hardest hit by the new vaccination mandate.
Enrollment in rural schools will drastically decline due to the new mandate.
School district budgets will crumble as a result of decreased enrollment in rural schools.
The learning and social losses in rural schools will be massive due to the vaccination mandate.
California's budget deficit has reached $68 billion.
Gavin Newsom's disapproval numbers have hit an all-time high.
Democrats' fiscal mismanagement has contributed to California's budget deficit.
In the last five years, California's spending has increased by more than 60%.
The increase in spending in California is described as unsustainable.
Californians' quality of life has collapsed.
People are moving from California to Florida.
California is allowing biological boys to compete in girls' sports.
Governor Newsom has referred to the situation in girls' sports as 'deeply unfair.'
National polling shows nearly 70% of Americans agree that biological males should not be allowed to compete in girls' events.
A third of Californians live in homeowners associations (HOAs).
Many HOAs are unable to meet the often very high quorum requirements needed to elect board members.
Current law prevents homeowners associations from certifying an election if a majority of homeowners do not turn in their ballots.
Board members in HOAs can resist challengers by nullifying elections, allowing them to remain in office for decades.
California public elections do not have a minimum voter turnout required before someone can be elected to office.
Gavin Newsom has disowned progressive policies he previously championed in California.
Newsom has used the term 'Latinx' repeatedly in speeches, official statements, and across social media.
Newsom previously praised California’s trans student law as a civil rights advancement.
CNN's investigation suggests that Newsom is acting like progressive issues he once supported were never legitimate.
Democrats in the California Legislature are reportedly mystified by Newsom's recent statements and actions.
The $50 million slush fund proposal could go to protecting illegal immigrants with felony convictions from deportation.
A letter in the legislative journal is NOT legally binding.
The bill could result in taxpayer funds going to the legal defense of undocumented immigrants who have been convicted of a crime.
Democrats rejected a safeguard to prohibit taxpayer-funded legal aid from helping felons.
The rejection of the safeguard is described as disingenuous by legal scholars.
Voters expressed a desire for lawmakers to crack down on crime in the November election.
AB 1189 expands the pool of qualified court reporters by certifying digital court reporters and legal transcriptionists.
California courts have held over 1.2 million hearings without a verbatim record between October 2023 and August 2024 due to a shortage of certified court reporters.
The shortage of certified court reporters particularly affects low-income or underserved communities, preventing them from appealing important decisions.
The legislation recognizes digital reporting as a valid and reliable method for capturing legal proceedings.
AB 1189 establishes a state-run certification process for digital court reporters and legal transcriptionists.
Certified digital reporters would be authorized to administer oaths and maintain chain-of-custody protocols for legal records.
California courts need 458 more full-time court reporters to meet current demand as of January 2025.
Nearly half of all active CSR licenses were issued more than 30 years ago, indicating a rapidly aging workforce.
Between January 2023 and September 2024, California courts experienced a net loss of court reporters despite aggressive hiring efforts.
Youth football is safer than ever in California under the passage of the California Youth Football Act – AB 1 in 2019.
California leads the nation in safety standards for youth sports.
AB 1 is the model for the rest of the country when it comes to what a youth sports safety bill should look like.
Banning youth sports follows a pattern of criminalization of activities that are central to marginalized black and brown communities.
The bill to ban youth football would disproportionately affect young people of color, especially young men.
California gas prices could soar 75% to $8.43 per gallon by 2026, driven by refinery shutdowns and the state’s overreach with excessive regulation and taxation.
The Low Carbon Fuel Standard (LCFS) regulations could raise gas prices by 65 cents per gallon.
The state gas tax has reached 61.2 cents per gallon, the highest gas tax in the nation.
The California Air Resources Board (CARB) has imposed additional costs in the fuel supply chain.
State-imposed costs will lead to unpredictable spikes in fuel prices throughout the summer and the foreseeable future.
The California Air Resources Board projected that an update to the Low Carbon Fuel Standard could raise gas prices by more than $0.50 cents a gallon.
Legislative Republicans allege that the California Air Resources Board is not being honest about the impact of proposed regulations on consumers.
The proposed changes to the cap-and-trade program could impose expensive new restrictions.
High gas prices in California are described as a basic necessity issue rather than a luxury.
If families pay more to fill up their gas tank, they will have less money available for food, bills, or savings.
The Newsom administration is facing criticism for potentially increasing costs for consumers.
Homelessness has shot up nearly 60% from 2014 to 2023.
Out of control retail theft continues to damage small businesses and drive up costs for consumers.
Proposition 47 has failed to protect communities and businesses.
There is a need to fix public safety problems by holding criminals accountable.
The Democratic leadership is trying to confuse the public about ballot initiatives that would result in stronger penalties for drug- and theft-related crimes.
La participación de los padres ayuda a enriquecer la experiencia de aprendizaje de los niños.
Fomentar la participación de los padres en el nivel preescolar es esencial para ayudar a los padres a adquirir habilidades y estrategias para la crianza.
El programa SB 725 busca apoyar programas preescolares diseñados para ayudar a los padres a aprender cómo apoyar el aprendizaje y desarrollo de sus hijos.
Las subvenciones del programa se utilizarían para salarios de maestros, aulas, currículos y desarrollo del aprendizaje de los niños.
The devastation from recent fires poses a significant threat to rural Californians.
Families affected by wildfires are potentially facing long-term health conditions due to smoke.
A comprehensive database to measure the specific health impacts of wildfires and the effectiveness of fuels mitigation investments is necessary.
Annual reporting on the impact of non-renewals within the Wildland Urban Interface (WUI) is required.
CEQA exemptions for defensible space projects can help property owners reduce wildfire risk more efficiently.
Sales and use tax exemptions on fire equipment purchases may encourage investment in wildfire response resources.
Access to funding for water districts in high fire hazard zones is critical for strengthening infrastructure for drought resilience and wildfire response.
Senate Bill 268 aims to close a legal loophole that fails to recognize the violent nature of the rape of an unconscious person.
Under current law, the rape of an intoxicated person is not classified as a violent felony.
Current legislation allows individuals convicted of non-violent felonies, including the rape of an intoxicated person, to be eligible for early prison release.
Senate Bill 268 seeks to classify drugging and rape as violent assaults.
The overwhelming support for SB 268 reflects the urgency of recognizing the rape of an intoxicated person as a violent felony.
Liberty Healthcare employs a placement strategy known as 'Hide the Predator,' where homes they lease for SVPs are kept secret from neighbors, school officials, and local law enforcement.
The California Legislature supports the need to audit Liberty Healthcare’s management of SVPs.
California's homeless population has increased nearly 40% in the past 6 years.
California has spent over $22 billion on the homelessness crisis.
California's homelessness crisis has become a public health and safety crisis.
The bipartisan Senate Bill 1011 aims to take the City of San Diego’s 'Unsafe Camping Ordinance' statewide.
Senate Bill 1011 will help compassionately clear encampments and connect homeless individuals to necessary services.
Senate Bill 467 will destroy 50,000+ jobs in California.
Senate Bill 467 will make California nearly 100% reliant on foreign energy imports.
Senate Bill 467 will raise gas prices to as much as $10 per gallon.
An estimated 40 million people globally have been forced into modern slavery through human trafficking over the last decade.
Human trafficking is one of the world's fastest growing and most lucrative crimes, generating around $150 billion each year.
Victims of human trafficking often suffer from long-term physical and psychological trauma.
The American Academy of Pediatrics has recognized the severe trauma that human trafficking victims experience.
Human traffickers exploit individuals through violence and manipulation, leading to lives of modern-day slavery.
California ranks number one in the nation for the number of human trafficking cases reported to the National Human Trafficking Hotline.
Law enforcement agencies have dedicated special task forces to combat human trafficking in California.
Stronger consequences are required to hold criminals accountable for human trafficking.
The introduction of SB 1042 would classify human trafficking as a strike under California's Three Strikes law.
There is a need for penalties for repeat offenders of human trafficking.
The legislation aims to prevent traffickers from receiving early parole.
Senate Bill 15 aims to encourage the production of environmentally responsible oil in California.
Much of the crude oil imported into California comes from foreign nations with demonstrated human rights abuses.
Senate Bill 15 would require the Air Resources Board to report on particulate matter released by tanker ships importing oil into California.
California consumes 1.8 million barrels of oil each day, of which less than 400,000 are produced by in-state workers.
California's demand for oil causes consumers to send more than $25 billion a year to other countries for imported oil.
Petroleum is used to produce more than 6,000 everyday products, including phones and heart valves.
According to the California Independent Petroleum Association, California's oil industry directly supports 55,000 jobs with an average annual pay of $123,000.
The average salary in California is $63,783 per year, approximately half of the average salary in the oil industry.
Senate Bill 15 aims to bring back jobs lost to foreign countries that do not respect environmental or democratic values.
The push for Permanent Standard Time over Daylight Saving Time is supported by evidence suggesting that standard time aligns more closely with human circadian rhythms.
Standard time is associated with fewer health risks, such as heart attacks and sleep disturbances.
SB 1381 expands the scope of child pornography laws to include material generated using artificial intelligence.
SB 1381 ensures that AI-generated explicit content involving minors is treated with the seriousness it deserves.
SB 1831 adds depictions of minors created with artificial intelligence to California’s existing child pornography laws.
SB 1831 aims to combat the growing threat of AI-generated explicit content involving minors.
As AI technology advances, existing laws must evolve to protect children from new forms of exploitation.
The California Department of Food and Agriculture’s Healthy Soils Program (HSP) awarded $99,954.38 to the Old Grove Orange organization.
The Healthy Soils Program is one of the most promising state agricultural programs currently implemented in California.
The Healthy Soils Program provides agriculturalists with grants to assist with the shift towards regenerative agriculture.
In its first year, the Healthy Soils Program awarded $41.5 million in grant funding for 646 regenerative farms in California.
PMMC is the only center in Orange County licensed to rescue and respond to sea lions, seals, sea turtles, dolphins, and whales.
PMMC has worked tirelessly to ensure the highest animal rescue and rehabilitation.
PMMC offers effective STEM educational programs.
PMMC conducts comprehensive research related to ocean stewardship.
SB 296 aims to exempt the primary residences of 100% disabled veterans from property taxes.
AB 30 will allow a more cost-efficient and environmentally friendly E15 blend of gasoline to be sold in California.
SB 571 seeks to protect victims from opportunistic criminals during a state of emergency by increasing penalties for looting or impersonating first responders.
SB 563 would expand access to needed grant funds for law enforcement who patrol mixed terrain areas throughout California.
SB 508 expands healthcare access for cancer patients who need it most.
SB 630 and AB 1138 help keep good paying jobs in California by providing incentives for TV and film production.
Senate Bill 405 would have allowed communities throughout California to authorize voter ID requirements for their local elections.
A survey by the Berkeley Institute of Government Studies found that a vast majority of Californians support requiring a government ID to vote.
As of 2024, 46.2% of California voters are registered as Democrats, while 24.7% are registered Republicans.
Each Republican Senator in California represents approximately 1 million constituents.
Numerous committees in the California State Senate only have one member from the minority party.
California is represented by a Democratic supermajority, which stifles representation for Republicans.
Synthetic opioids, like fentanyl, are the number one killer for Americans aged 18-45.
The Orange County Coroners Division reported 717 fentanyl related deaths in 2021.
In Orange County, there was a 162% increase in fentanyl related deaths from 2019 to 2020.
California is home to America’s most homeless people despite spending more than $20 billion of taxpayer dollars under Governor Newsom's leadership.
California lacks the water storage capacity to capture water from atmospheric river storms, impacting farmers and farmworkers.
Governor Newsom's proposed 'Windfall Profits Tax' on the gasoline industry would increase the cost of gasoline at the pump.
Two-thirds of California students failed to meet Math standards according to statewide test scores.
Stanford University Hoover Institution Study reveals that businesses are leaving California twice as fast as in previous years.
Governor Newsom's Employment Development Department sent out $33 billion in unemployment fraud during the disorganized effort to provide benefits.
An investigation found that Governor Newsom overstated the number of acres treated with fuel breaks and prescribed burns by 690%.
The actual number of acres treated as a result of Governor Newsom's executive order was only 11,399 acres, contrary to his claim of 90,000 acres.
Governor Newsom has been criticized for his inconsistent stance on the High-Speed Rail project, leading to significant taxpayer expenditures.
